# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: log-agent-deploy
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: myapp
  replicas: 3   # number of pod instances you want to run (default is 2)
  template:     # Pod Template specification. This will be used for creating newPod as a clone/copy using the spec field below and create multiple copies if needed, or use it directly with .spec instead which allows more control over creation process beyond just replicas count above  
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: myapp  # Labels to identify pod instances (default is 'my-pod')
    spec:      
      containers:     # Define the container(s) that will be created by this Pod. Each Container in a Pod should have its own image and command for it or else default ones are used if not specified below  
        - name: logagent  # Name of each individual Docker instance (default is 'my-container')   
          image: myapp/log_agents     # Image to use, you can add more images as per your requirement. If no specific docker logs agent found then default one will be used  
      volumes:        # Define the volume(s) that should exist within pod instances (default is empty if not specified below). The contents of these Volumes are managed by Kubernetes and do NOT need to manually created or deleted, they only affect what data gets written into them. If no specific host directories found then default one will be used  
        - name: log-volume  # Name for each volume (default is 'my-logs')   
          persistentVolumeClaim:     # Claims are objects that describe a PVC to which the pod has access and in what state. Kubernetes uses these claims as an abstract representation of storage requested by Pod or Controller  
            claimName: pvc-name  # Name for existing Persistent Volume Claim (default is 'my-pvc')   
          mountPath: /var/log     # Path where the data should be mounted within pod instance. The directory must exist at container startup and Kubernetes will manage it  
            readOnly: false      # Whether to enable or disable access from outside this Pod, only set if you want an isolated environment (default is true)   
        - name: lib-volume  # Name for each volume    
          persistentVolumeClaim:      
              claimName: pvc2   # Existing Persistent Volume Claim's data will be managed by Kubernetes as a PVC and linked to the defined mountPath within pod instances. The directory must exist at container startup and can only access via this Pod (default is 'my-pv')   
          mountPath: /var/lib/docker   # Path where log agent should write logs into inside each Docker instance, Kubernetes will manage it    
            readOnly: false      # Whether to enable or disable logging for the container(s) within this Pod. Only set if you want an isolated environment (default is true).    If not specified then default value 'false'
